2606.06454.title: Scaffold, Not Vocabulary? A Controlled, Two-Tier, Pre-Registered Study of a Popperian Code-Generation Skill
2606.06454.paper_summary: Large language models increasingly write, review, and judge code, and a fast-growing practice equips them with prompt 'skills' that ask the model to reason like a scientist. A prominent example tells the model to act as a Popperian falsificationist, and such skills are reported to improve generated code. But these gains are almost always read off an LLM-as-a-judge, an instrument with documented positional, self-preference, and stylistic biases. We ask: if it appears to help, is the gain from the skill's Popperian content, or from the structure any scaffold imposes? We pre-register a two-tier ablation with three controls: a length-matched placebo, a labels-only scaffold that keeps the Popperian headers but strips the procedure, and an execution oracle (HumanEval+ unit tests), plus a vocabulary-halo sentinel and a same-model self-judge audit. On a frontier model (Claude Sonnet 4.6, N=163) all conditions sit near the benchmark ceiling and do not separate, so the pre-registered +5-point improvement is not supported (a ceiling-limited non-detection). On a small model (Qwen2.5-Coder-0.5B, N=164) structured arms lift best-of-eight correctness by 20-22 points, but the full skill shows no separable benefit over a labels-only scaffold (aggregate F@8=L@8 vs V@8=34.8%), and the placebo trails by only 2.4 points. A 0.5B self-judge applying the Popperian rubric does not beat random selection and concentrates 60% of its picks on one index. In the two settings tested, the skill's Popperian procedural content adds no separable execution-correctness benefit beyond a labels-only scaffold, so the gains track scaffold structure. We contribute a calibrated negative result and a reusable disambiguation protocol; the finding bounds an engineering claim about one prompt-skill family and is not an evaluation of Popperian methodology in general.
2606.06454.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地撰寫、審查和評判代碼，並且一種快速增長的實踐使它們具備了提示“技能”，要求模型像科學家一樣推理。一個顯著的例子告訴模型要作為一個波普式的反駁主義者行事，據報導這些技能可以改善生成的代碼。但這些增益幾乎總是從 LLM 作為評判者的角度來解讀，這是一種具有文獻記載的定位、自我偏好和風格偏見的工具。我們問：如果看起來有幫助，這種增益是來自技能的波普式內容，還是來自任何支架所施加的結構？我們預註冊了一個兩層的消融實驗，並設置了三個對照組：一個長度匹配的安慰劑、一個僅包含標籤的支架，保留波普式的標題但刪除程序，以及一個執行預言者（HumanEval+ 單元測試），再加上一個詞彙光環哨兵和同模型自我評判的審計。在一個前沿模型（Claude Sonnet 4.6, N=163）中，所有條件都接近基準上限且無法區分，因此預註冊的 +5 分改進未得到支持（一個上限限制的非檢測）。在一個小型模型（Qwen2.5-Coder-0.5B, N=164）中，結構化的武器將最佳的八項正確性提高了 20-22 分，但完整的技能顯示在僅標籤的支架上沒有可分離的好處（總體 F@8=L@8 與 V@8=34.8%），而安慰劑僅落後 2.4 分。一個 0.5B 的自我評判者應用波普式標準並未超過隨機選擇，並將 60% 的選擇集中在一個指標上。在測試的兩個設置中，技能的波普式程序內容在超出僅標籤支架的執行正確性方面未增加可分離的好處，因此增益與支架結構相關。我們提供了一個經過校準的負面結果和一個可重用的消歧義協議；這一發現界定了一個關於某個提示技能家族的工程聲明，而不是對波普式方法論的一般評估。
2606.06454.author: Mehmet Iscan et.al.
